title: Tabduct tool catalog
description: Single source of truth for the MVP tool set. Hosts read this to register MCP tools; the extension reads it to know what it must implement. protocolVersion is the wire-protocol version (see PROTOCOL.md).

tools.name: list_tabs
tools.description: List open browser tabs (id, title, url, whether active/window).

tools.name: get_active_tab
tools.description: Return the currently focused tab (id, title, url).

tools.name: open_tab
tools.description: Open a new tab, optionally at a URL. Returns the new tab.

url.description: Optional URL; omit for a blank tab.

tools.name: activate_tab
tools.description: Bring a tab (and its window) to the foreground.

tools.name: navigate
tools.description: Navigate a tab to a URL. Defaults to the active tab.

tabId.description: Target tab; omit for active tab.

waitUntilComplete.description: Resolve only after the load finishes (bounded by an internal deadline).

tools.name: get_page_content
tools.description: Read a tab's content: visible innerText, logical textContent, rendered HTML, or full outerHTML.

tabId.description: Target tab; omit for active tab.

maxChars.description: Truncate result to this many chars (0 = no limit).

tools.name: execute_script
tools.description: Run JavaScript in a tab and return the (JSON-serializable) result. Keystone tool: read AND modify the page. NOTE: your code must `return` its result (the wrapper is a statement body). On strict-CSP sites, MAIN-world eval may be blocked (see error code CSP_BLOCKED); the roadmap fix is chrome.userScripts. See ROADMAP.md.

code.description: JS source; must `return` the value to hand back. Runs in the page (MAIN) world. Result is capped at 8MB (truncated flag set if exceeded).

tabId.description: Target tab; omit for active tab.

args.description: Optional JSON-serializable args exposed as `args` to the code.

engine.description: auto=chrome.scripting, fall back to CDP on CSP_BLOCKED if CDP is enabled; cdp=force CDP (requires the debugger permission + 'Allow CDP eval').

tools.name: screenshot
tools.description: Capture the VISIBLE area of a tab as an image (returned to the agent as an MCP image). To capture a specific part of a long page, first scroll it into view: pass `selector` (scroll an element into view) or `scrollTo` (scroll to a Y offset), then it captures the resulting viewport. (Whole-page/full-length capture is intentionally not supported — it is unreliable on modern virtualized/infinite pages; scroll-and-shoot instead.) Only the ACTIVE tab of a window can be captured; pass a background tabId with activate=true to bring it forward first.

tabId.description: Target tab; omit for active tab.

activate.description: If the tab is not active, activate it before capturing (side effect: changes focus).

selector.description: Scroll this element into view before capturing the viewport.

scrollTo.description: Scroll to this vertical pixel offset before capturing the viewport.

tools.name: wait_for
tools.description: Wait until a condition is met in a shared tab (a CSS selector appears, the URL contains a substring, or the page reaches a load state), bounded by a timeout. At least one of selector/urlContains/loadState is required. CSP-safe: uses injected functions, never string eval (so it works on strict-CSP sites).

tabId.description: Target tab; omit for active tab.

selector.description: CSS selector that must match an element before resolving.

urlContains.description: Resolve when location.href contains this substring.

loadState.description: Resolve when document.readyState equals this (only 'complete' is supported).

timeoutMs.description: Max time to wait in ms; capped at 25000. Resolves as soon as a condition holds; throws TIMEOUT otherwise.

tools.name: click
tools.description: Click an element in a shared tab by CSS selector. CSP-safe: injects a function (page CSP blocks string eval, not extension-injected functions).

tabId.description: Target tab; omit for active tab.

selector.description: CSS selector of the element to click.

tools.name: type
tools.description: Type text into an input/textarea/contenteditable element in a shared tab by CSS selector. Dispatches input/change events so frameworks (React/Vue) notice the change. CSP-safe (injected function).

tabId.description: Target tab; omit for active tab.

selector.description: CSS selector of the element to type into.

clear.description: Replace the current value (true) instead of appending (false).

tools.name: get_dom_snapshot
tools.description: Return a compact text outline of a shared tab's visible interactive/structural elements (a/button/input/textarea/select/[role]/headings/nav/form etc.), each with a label and a reasonably stable CSS selector — enough to choose click/type targets on strict-CSP sites without arbitrary JS. CSP-safe (injected function).

tabId.description: Target tab; omit for active tab.

maxChars.description: Truncate the outline to this many chars (0 = no limit, hard cap 200000).

tools.name: get_console_logs
tools.description: Return console logs captured from a shared tab (log/info/warn/error/debug). When the user has enabled 'Capture full console & errors via CDP' (developer mode), this returns the FULL CDP-captured picture: console.* calls, uncaught exceptions, and browser Log entries (network/CSP/deprecation), recorded continuously from the moment the tab was shared — including errors emitted before you asked. Otherwise it falls back to best-effort injected console capture: installed on the FIRST call (so logs emitted before that call may be missing) and reset when the page navigates. The capture source is reported in the result's `source` field ('cdp' or 'inject'). CSP-safe: neither path uses string eval.

tabId.description: Target tab; omit for active tab.

clear.description: Clear the captured buffer after returning it.

tools.name: list_network_requests
tools.description: List network requests captured from a shared tab (method, url, status, resource type, size, duration). Requires the user to have enabled 'Capture console, errors & network via CDP' (developer mode) — otherwise returns an empty list with source:'off' and a hint. Recorded continuously from the moment the tab was shared. Results are newest-last; pass a returned requestId to get_network_request for headers and the response body. CSP-safe (CDP).

tabId.description: Target tab; omit for active tab.

urlContains.description: Only requests whose URL contains this substring.

resourceType.description: Filter by resource type (Document, XHR, Fetch, Script, Stylesheet, Image, Font, Media, WebSocket, …), case-insensitive.

statusMin.description: Only responses with HTTP status >= this (e.g. 400 to see failures).

limit.description: Return at most this many, most-recent first-dropped (i.e. the newest `limit`).

clear.description: Clear the captured buffer after returning it.

tools.name: get_network_request
tools.description: Return full detail for ONE captured network request by its requestId (from list_network_requests): request/response headers, status, timing, remote IP, and (optionally) the response body. Requires network capture to be enabled and the tab still attached. The body may be base64 for binary responses and is size-capped; it can be unavailable if Chrome has already evicted it from its buffer. SECURITY: headers and bodies may contain credentials (Authorization, Cookie, Set-Cookie, tokens) — treat the result as secret and never echo it to an untrusted destination.

tabId.description: Target tab; omit for active tab.

includeBody.description: Also fetch the response body (may fail if the body is no longer buffered).

maxBodyBytes.description: Truncate the body to this many bytes. 0 = use the 2MB hard cap (the hard cap always applies).
